notes: Signatures are represented as boolean 'signature_valid' to keep vectors runnable without live Ed25519. Implementers replace with real signatures when integrating with their crypto stack. Vectors cover §7.3 scope-match table, V3-1 defense, §3.1 Layer-1 narrowness-rule (wildcards in cap require budget.actions ≤ narrowness_threshold, default 20), and R7 tightenings (design-round7-tightenings.md) for role hierarchy (R7.D1), chain depth (R7.D3), integration wildcards (R7.D4), and empty-cap mandates (R7.D5). 'context' field simulates the Router's state: current Tariff version, current time, active revocation entries, narrowness_threshold. Post-R7 status: ds-021 rejects direct root→mandate_signer (role-hierarchy-violation); ds-054 rejects integrations=['*'] (scope-integrations-wildcard-forbidden); ds-055 rejects empty-cap (mandate-empty-cap); ds-057 rejects 5-key chain (chain-depth-exceeded). All OPEN SPEC QUESTION markers in bounded areas (role hierarchy, chain depth, wildcards, empty-cap) are resolved; operational questions (valid_until inclusivity, max_tier_signable=0 semantics) remain documented per-vector.

vectors.description: Single-link delegation: ops key directly signs mandate within declared integration and tier
vectors.rationale: Exact-match happy path. All scope fields satisfied. §7.3 table passes all rows. Establishes baseline accept behavior.

vectors.description: Two-link chain: root → ops → mandate, all scopes match
vectors.rationale: Full chain walk per §7.4. Every link valid; every scope-match row passes. Tests that multi-link verification is implemented, not just last-link.

vectors.description: Mandate uses multiple caps, all within scope
vectors.rationale: Multiple caps. max(tiers) rule uses highest tier (3). §7.3 requires max(mandate.cap.tiers) ≤ scope.max_tier_signable.

vectors.description: Wildcard verb and resource_kind in delegation scope; mandate uses specific verb and kind
vectors.rationale: Wildcard allows any concrete verb/kind. Tests wildcard semantics in allowlist.

vectors.description: Mandate tier strictly below max_tier_signable
vectors.rationale: Strict inequality tier: 2 < 4. Accept. Differentiates from valid-boundary at-limit case.

vectors.description: Mandate budget strictly below max_budget
vectors.rationale: Budget 10/1000 < 100/50000. Both dimensions below.

vectors.description: Mandate exp window shorter than max_exp_seconds allows
vectors.rationale: exp-issued_at = 3600 < 86400. Accept.

vectors.description: Mandate uses subset of allowed integrations
vectors.rationale: integration_ref ∈ {k8s-prod, vault-prod, stripe-prod}. Subset membership.

vectors.description: Mandate uses subset of allowed verbs
vectors.rationale: Both patch and get ∈ {patch, apply, delete, get}. Tests multi-cap verb-by-verb membership check.

vectors.description: Nested delegation narrows each dimension; mandate well within innermost
vectors.rationale: Both delegation links strict-narrower than parent; mandate strict-narrower than innermost. §7.4 walks chain bottom-up.

vectors.description: Mandate tier exactly equals max_tier_signable
vectors.rationale: Boundary test: tier 3 ≤ max_tier_signable 3 (inclusive). §7.3 uses ≤, not <.

vectors.description: Mandate budget exactly equals max_budget
vectors.rationale: budget.actions==max.actions AND budget.tokens==max.tokens. §7.3 inequalities are inclusive.

vectors.description: exp - issued_at exactly equals max_exp_seconds
vectors.rationale: exp - issued_at = 14400 == max_exp_seconds 14400. §7.3 uses ≤.

vectors.description: Delegation valid_until exactly equals current_time
vectors.rationale: OPEN SPEC QUESTION: is valid_until inclusive or exclusive? This vector specifies EXCLUSIVE (delegation expires AT valid_until, not after). Implementations must match. If spec differs, this is a spec ambiguity to raise.

vectors.description: Mandate signature does not verify
vectors.rationale: Basic signature check. Precedes scope checks; MUST fail fast on signature.

vectors.description: Root→ops delegation signature broken
vectors.rationale: Chain integrity. Even if mandate sig is valid, a broken upstream link must reject.

vectors.description: Intermediate ops→mandate delegation signature broken, mandate sig valid
vectors.rationale: Any link in chain broken → overall reject. Tests that multi-link verification does not stop at mandate signature.

vectors.description: Delegation signed_by claims different key than parent_key
vectors.rationale: self-signing attack: delegation claims parent_key=K_cust_root but signed_by=K_cust_ops (itself, the child). Implementation MUST check signed_by matches parent_key. Defense against V3-1-adjacent chain-tampering.

vectors.description: Empty delegation chain
vectors.rationale: No delegation → signer key not derivable from root. Must reject. Defense: self-signed mandate trying to pass as root-derived.

vectors.description: Chain does not terminate at K_cust_root (first link's parent_key is not trust anchor)
vectors.rationale: Trust anchor pinning: top of chain must be K_cust_root_pk_TEST (Router-pinned). Rogue root MUST reject even with valid self-sig.

vectors.description: Chain has gap: ops-level delegation missing, mandate-signer's delegation claims root as parent but root didn't actually delegate to mandate-signer directly
vectors.rationale: R7.D1 (design-round7-tightenings.md): direct root→mandate_signer delegation is FORBIDDEN. The three-level hierarchy K_cust_root → K_cust_ops → K_cust_mandate_* is normative; Router MUST reject any chain that skips the ops level for mandate_signer terminal role. This closes a V3-1 defense-in-depth gap: ops-key compromise cascades only to ops-signable actions, not to mandate authority.

vectors.description: Mandate signer_key_hint does not match last delegation's child_key
vectors.rationale: Mandate signer must match last delegation's child. Any mismatch breaks chain. Defends against attacker with valid signature on one key trying to use chain of a different key.

vectors.description: Chain has duplicate link (same delegation appears twice)
vectors.rationale: Malformed chain (duplicate link) must reject. Prevents ambiguity in scope intersection.

vectors.description: Mandate integration_ref 'vault-prod' not in delegation's allowed integrations ['k8s-prod']
vectors.rationale: THE V3-1 attack: mandate signer has ops-scoped-to-k8s key but tries to use for vault integration. §7.3 integration check catches this. Prototype V3-1 defense test.

vectors.description: Mandate integration_ref not in any link of multi-link chain
vectors.rationale: stripe-prod not in innermost scope {k8s-prod}. §7.3 applied at each link; innermost controls.

vectors.description: Mandate integration in outer scope but not innermost
vectors.rationale: vault-prod ∈ outer but not ∈ innermost. CORE V3-1 defense: narrow key must not escape narrow delegation even if outer permits wider.

vectors.description: Case-sensitive integration match: 'K8S-PROD' should not match 'k8s-prod'
vectors.rationale: Integration IDs are case-sensitive byte strings. 'K8S-PROD' != 'k8s-prod'. §4.2 normalization forbids case-variation. Defends against normalization-bypass scope-drift.

vectors.description: Mandate tier=4 exceeds delegation max_tier_signable=3
vectors.rationale: Tier 4 > max_tier_signable 3. §7.3 row: max(mandate.cap.tiers) ≤ scope.max_tier_signable.

vectors.description: One cap in multi-cap list exceeds tier; others within
vectors.rationale: max(tiers) = 3 > 2. Any single cap exceeding reject entire mandate (atomic).

vectors.description: Innermost scope limits tier; outermost would allow
vectors.rationale: Tier 4 ≤ outer 5 but > inner 2. Inner scope binding. CORE V3-1 test.

vectors.description: Mandate tier exactly one above limit (off-by-one)
vectors.rationale: Tier 3, max 2. Differentiates valid-boundary ds-011 from off-by-one failure.

vectors.description: Mandate verb 'delete' not in allowed_verbs ['patch', 'apply']
vectors.rationale: 'delete' ∉ {patch, apply}. §7.3 scope-match.

vectors.description: One cap in multi-cap mandate has forbidden verb
vectors.rationale: Any forbidden verb rejects whole mandate. No partial acceptance.

vectors.description: Verb case-variation: 'PATCH' (upper) when allowed is 'patch' (lower)
vectors.rationale: Mandate caps MUST already be canonical. Verbs are lowercased per §4.2 normalization BEFORE classifier. Mandate-time non-canonical verb rejected at scope check. Complements canonicalization.json.

vectors.description: Empty allowed_verbs → all concrete verbs rejected
vectors.rationale: Empty list = no verbs allowed. Must not be interpreted as wildcard.

vectors.description: Mandate resource_kind 'pod' not in allowed_resource_kinds ['deployment', 'service']
vectors.rationale: pod ∉ {deployment, service}. §7.3 scope-match.

vectors.description: Synonym/alias bypass attempt: 'deployments.apps' not equal canonical 'deployment'
vectors.rationale: Resource kinds in mandates MUST already be in canonical form (per §4.2 — normalization applies before reaching scope-check). 'deployments.apps' is a K8s discovery alias; canonical form is 'deployment'. Non-canonical form in cap rejected.

vectors.description: Empty allowed_resource_kinds
vectors.rationale: Empty list ≠ wildcard. Parallel to ds-035 for verbs.

vectors.description: Mandate budget.actions exceeds delegation max_budget.actions
vectors.rationale: 101 > 100. Off-by-one. Pairs with ds-012 boundary.

vectors.description: Mandate budget.tokens exceeds delegation max_budget.tokens (actions within)
vectors.rationale: Tokens exceed even though actions within. Any dimension exceeding rejects. AND semantics.

vectors.description: Nested: innermost budget limits 50; outermost 1000; mandate 100
vectors.rationale: 100 ≤ 1000 (outer) but > 50 (inner). Innermost controls.

vectors.description: Budget field negative (implementation must reject as malformed)
vectors.rationale: Negative actions nonsensical; must reject (CBOR 'uint' validation would catch, JSON source may not). Defense against modular-wrap interpretation.

vectors.description: exp - issued_at = 15000, max_exp_seconds = 14400
exp earlier than issued_at (nonsense; reject as malformed)
vectors.rationale: Mandate already expired at issuance time (exp < issued_at). Logical impossibility. OPEN SPEC QUESTION: should reject_code be 'expired' or 'scope-expiry-malformed'? This vector picks 'expired' since effect is same.

vectors.description: max_exp_seconds = 0 should reject any mandate
vectors.rationale: 3600 > 0. Delegation effectively disables mandate-signing for duration > 0. Edge case for zero-duration scopes.

vectors.description: current_time past delegation valid_until
vectors.rationale: Delegation expired before mandate issued. §7.4 link validity check.

vectors.description: current_time past mandate exp (mandate itself expired)
vectors.rationale: current_time 1712100000 > mandate exp 1712003600. Mandate itself expired.

vectors.description: current_time before delegation valid_from (not yet active)
vectors.rationale: Delegation not yet active. 'expired' reused as generic time-out-of-window code. OPEN SPEC QUESTION: separate code 'not-yet-valid' may be warranted.

vectors.description: K_cust_mandate_N in revocation list
vectors.rationale: Key on revocation list → reject regardless of signature/time validity.

vectors.description: Specific mandate revoked by mandate_id
vectors.rationale: Mandate-specific revocation (vs key-specific). Router checks both.

vectors.description: Root revoked — any descendant invalidated
vectors.rationale: Root compromise → all descendants invalidated. §7.6 root-compromise recovery relies on this.

vectors.description: Mandate min_tariff_version > current
vectors.rationale: Mandate requires Tariff v10 but Router has v5. §3.2 mandate-Tariff binding.

vectors.description: Mandate min_tariff_version = current (boundary — accept)
vectors.rationale: Boundary: min_version = current. §3.2 uses ≥. Pairs with ds-052 off-by-one test.

vectors.description: Delegation with all wildcards — maximally permissive (still accept)
vectors.rationale: R7.D4 (design-round7-tightenings.md): `integrations: ["*"]` is FORBIDDEN at every delegation level. Integrations MUST be explicitly enumerated. Wildcard at delegation would grant authority for any integration the Tariff ever adds, defeating the per-integration scope-match defense (§7.3). V3-1 defense strengthened by making integration enumeration orthogonal to verb/resource-kind enumeration.

vectors.description: Mandate with empty cap list (no capabilities at all)
vectors.rationale: R7.D5 (design-round7-tightenings.md): mandates with `cap: []` are FORBIDDEN. An empty-cap mandate authorizes nothing yet carries signature authority in the audit chain. Forbidding removes an attack-surface primitive (placeholder mandate mutated post-issuance). Distinct from `scope-verb-forbidden` which fires on specific verb absent; this is a structural rejection at mandate format check.

vectors.description: Delegation with max_tier_signable = 0 (only Tier 0 can be signed — useless for §2+)
vectors.rationale: OPEN SPEC QUESTION: §2 says Tier 0-1 don't require mandates. But nothing forbids signing one. This vector accepts for consistency. A stricter interpretation might reject with 'mandate-unnecessary' or similar; flip if spec clarifies.

vectors.description: Delegation chain of length 5 (deep nesting)
vectors.rationale: R7.D3 (design-round7-tightenings.md): max chain depth = 4 keys / 3 links. This chain has 5 keys (root + L1..L4 + mandate_signer) = exceeds max. Rejected to prevent deep-chain complexity attacks and to keep validator runtime O(bounded). V3-1 defense: bounded chain depth makes scope-drift analysis tractable.

vectors.description: Delegation scope missing fields (schema violation)
vectors.rationale: Malformed DelegationDocument (missing scope fields max_budget, max_exp_seconds, allowed_verbs, allowed_resource_kinds). Router MUST reject at deserialization/validation, not default missing fields to permissive values.

vectors.description: V3-1 canonical attack: mandate-signer key scoped to k8s-prod, attacker tries to use for vault-prod mandate
vectors.rationale: EXACT V3-1 attack scenario. k8s-scoped key used for vault mandate. If Router only checks signature, accepts. §7.3 scope-match MUST reject. This vector is the canonical V3-1 defense test.

vectors.description: V3-1 variant: tier escalation via signer-hop — narrow tier-2 key used for tier-3 mandate
vectors.rationale: Tier-2-scoped key used to sign Tier-3 mandate. §7.3 tier row. Different form of V3-1: escalation not by integration but by tier.

vectors.description: V3-1 variant: budget expansion via sibling chain
vectors.rationale: Attacker with narrow-budget key tries to sign wide-budget mandate. Both actions (1000 > 10) and tokens (100000 > 1000) exceed.

vectors.description: V3-1 variant: expiry elongation attack — short-lived key signs long-lived mandate
vectors.rationale: 14400 > 300. Attacker with 5-minute-scoped key tries to sign 4-hour mandate. Escalates damage-window.

vectors.description: V3-1 variant: verb allowlist bypass — scope allows only read verbs, mandate requests write
vectors.rationale: Read-only-scoped key used for destructive delete. High-impact V3-1 variant.

vectors.description: V3-1 variant: resource-kind forgery — deployment-scoped key used for secret access
vectors.rationale: Key scoped to {deployment, service} used for secret. Integration_ref permits both, but resource_kind doesn't. Fine-grained V3-1 defense.

vectors.description: §3.1 Layer 1 narrowness-rule: mandate has wildcard '*' in cap.verb but budget.actions=100 exceeds narrowness_threshold default of 20.
vectors.rationale: design-final.md §3.1 Layer 1: wildcard '*' in cap.verb requires budget.actions ≤ narrowness_threshold (default 20). 100 > 20 → reject. This check is at mandate acceptance, distinct from the §7.3 scope-match table (which only verifies against delegation). V3-1+R2-CROSS-TIER-AGGREGATION defense; narrow-or-broad-not-both principle.

vectors.description: §3.1 narrowness-rule: cap.resource_ref is wildcard 'ns/*/*' but budget.actions=50 exceeds narrowness_threshold 20.
vectors.rationale: §3.1: wildcard in any position of cap (verb, resource_kind, or resource_ref) triggers the narrowness budget cap. resource_ref='ns/*/*' matches all namespaces + all pods — broad → budget.actions must ≤ 20. 50 > 20 → reject. Defense against a single-mandate cluster-wide pod delete (aggregation to Tier 4+).

vectors.description: §3.1 boundary: wildcard verb with budget.actions exactly at narrowness_threshold (20) — accepts.
vectors.rationale: §3.1 boundary: budget.actions=20 equals narrowness_threshold=20. Spec says ≤ threshold, so equality accepts. Pairs with ds-065 for ≤-vs-< disambiguation; drives spec precision on boundary inclusivity.

vectors.description: §3.1 narrowness: no wildcard anywhere in cap, but budget.actions is very high. Narrowness rule does NOT fire because cap is fully-qualified.
vectors.rationale: §3.1 applies only when cap contains wildcard. Fully-qualified cap (verb=get, resource_kind=pod, resource_ref=specific-pod) may have any budget within delegation's max_budget. 1000 ≤ 5000 delegation cap → accepts. Distinguishes §3.1 narrowness from §7.3 budget-subsumption.

vectors.description: Phase C.1 live-crypto vector: a two-link delegation chain (root → ops → mandate-signer) with every link and the mandate signed for real with Ed25519 COSE_Sign1. All three signatures MUST verify live against the per-vector trust anchors for the vector to accept.
vectors.rationale: design-final.md §7.3 scope-match + §7.3.1 depth cap: the canonical minimum happy-path chain. Exercises scope-match at both hops AND the mandate's own signature end-to-end under live crypto, which is the path mocks could only approximate.

vectors.description: Phase C.1 live-crypto vector: two-link chain with valid signatures, but the mandate's payload has been flipped in one byte after signing. Live verify on the mandate envelope MUST fail with signature-invalid even though the chain links themselves are intact.
vectors.rationale: design-final.md §2.2 + §7.3: the mandate MAC is independent from the chain MACs. A tamper on mandate bytes must fail at the mandate-signature step and never leak through via a successful chain walk.
